_comment: Forma Blog Content Calendar — controls automated post generation
_rotation: Categories cycle in order. AI picks a fresh angle each time, never repeating existing titles.

category_rotation.label: Training Science / HRV
category_rotation.description: Heart rate variability, periodisation, aerobic adaptation, VO2 max, lactate threshold, training zones, progressive overload, polarised training

category_rotation.label: Recovery & Sleep
category_rotation.description: Sleep quality and athletic performance, active recovery, parasympathetic nervous system, overreaching vs overtraining, deload weeks, nutrition for recovery, stress hormones and cortisol

category_rotation.label: Wearables & Data
category_rotation.description: Garmin, Oura, WHOOP, Apple Watch, Polar, COROS — accuracy, metrics comparison, HRV measurement differences, GPS tracking, chest strap vs optical HR, making sense of readiness scores

category_rotation.label: Race Prep & Performance
category_rotation.description: Tapering strategies, race day nutrition, pacing, half marathon and marathon prep, cycling race readiness, brick sessions, multi-sport periodisation, peak performance timing

tone_guidelines: Forma's voice is direct, science-backed, and anti-hype. Write like a smart coach who has read the research, not a journalist. Use real numbers and cite mechanisms. Avoid motivational fluff. The reader is a committed amateur endurance athlete — runner or cyclist — who trains 5-10 hours per week and wants to understand their body, not just be told what to do.
content_guidelines: Open with a strong counterintuitive hook or a common mistake
Include at least one concrete example or case (e.g. 'an athlete with a CTL of 80...')
Use subheadings to break up sections — readers scan first
End with a clear, actionable takeaway tied to the Forma readiness system
Do not use motivational clichés like 'push through', 'dig deep', 'no pain no gain'
Avoid generic advice — be specific (e.g. don't say 'sleep more', say 'aim for 8-9h in the 3 nights before a key session')
seo_guidelines: Target long-tail keywords naturally — e.g. 'how to use HRV for marathon training' not just 'HRV'
Meta description: 150-160 chars, includes primary keyword, no clickbait
Include internal context: mention Forma's readiness score where it naturally fits
